| **Primary**                       | **Treatment initiation date + biologically plausible lag** (e.g., 7–28 days depending on drug class) |
| **Alternative (for sensitivity)** | **Diagnosis date**, if modeling natural history or intent-to-treat cohort                            |
> 🧠 **Justification**: Aligns with onset of biological treatment effect and avoids immortal time bias from pre-treatment period.
---
### **2. End of Follow-up**
| **End Event**                   | **Definition**                                                          |
| ------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Death**                       | First confirmed death record in EHR, claims, or registry                |
| **Loss to follow-up**           | Last clinical encounter or prescription + 90 days without recontact     |
| **End of study period**         | Administrative censoring on **31 December 2022**                        |
| **Competing event (e.g., SCT)** | Treated as censoring or competing risk, depending on the analysis model |
---
### **3. Censoring Rules**
* **Death**: Event for primary analysis (not censored).
* **SCT / treatment switch**:
  * **On-treatment models**: Censor at event date.
  * **Intent-to-treat models**: Continue follow-up regardless of switch.
* **Administrative censoring**: At end of data availability or study period.
---
## 🔄 **Time-Varying Exposure Handling via Landmark Analysis**
### 🕰 **Landmark Analysis Design**
* Define fixed time points (e.g., **3 months**, **6 months**, **12 months**) post-treatment.
* At each landmark, include only those:
  * Alive and in active follow-up
  * With updated treatment status
* Assess outcomes from that point forward, reducing bias from immortal time or early dropout.
> 🔧 Used especially for **treatment switching**, **combination therapies**, or **progression** events.
---
## 🔙 **Left-Truncation Handling**
| **Issue**                                                           | **Solution**                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Entry into cohort after disease diagnosis or registry inclusion lag | Use **delayed entry models** where person-time starts at actual cohort entry (e.g., first prescription date + lag) |
| Reverse time bias                                                   | Apply **left-truncated survival models** (e.g., conditional risk sets in Cox model)                                |
| Missing early history                                               | Require ≥12 months of prior data to ensure eligibility                                                             |
> 📏 **Check** data completeness before start date to ensure valid time zero is captured.
---
### ✅ **Conclusion**
| Element         | Design Decision                                                                         |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Start**       | Treatment start + exposure lag (7–28 days)                                              |
| **End**         | Death, LTFU, or end of study (2022)                                                     |
| **Censor**      | For SCT or switching (in on-treatment models)                                           |
| **Adjustments** | Landmark analysis for time-varying regimens; left-truncation using delayed-entry models |

Q9:
### ✅ **Temporal Windows for Covariate Assessment in:**
**“DARWIN EU® CC – Multiple Myeloma: Patient Characterisation, Treatments, and Survival (2012–2022)”**
---
## 🧠 **Chain-of-Thought (CoT) Reasoning**
### 🎯 **Objective**
To define covariate assessment windows that:
* Reflect pre-treatment baseline (static),
* Capture ongoing treatment-related change (time-varying),
* Ensure causal order with outcomes (lagged),
  While complying with:
* **FDA SPL standards** (structured data windows),
* **Marginal Structural Models** (MSMs) for time-varying adjustment,
* **Defined thresholds for missing data imputation**.
---
## ⏳ **Window Strategy**
### 🟩 **Static Covariates**
Assessed at **baseline** and held constant throughout follow-up.
| **Covariate**      | **Window**                 | **Standard (SPL/EMA)**              | **Rationale**                                      |
| ------------------ | -------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| **Age, Sex**       | At **index date**          | FDA SPL: Baseline demographic block | Immutable demographic traits                       |
| **Comorbidities**  | **12 months pre-index**    | EMA: Baseline history requirement   | Needed to calculate Charlson or Elixhauser indices |
| **Race/Ethnicity** | At index or registry entry | FDA: Structured race metadata       | Baseline stratification                            |
> 🔧 **Default window = \[-365 days, 0] before treatment start**
---
### 🟨 **Time-Varying Covariates**
Assessed **periodically throughout follow-up** to capture treatment-related dynamics (e.g., labs, adverse events).
| **Covariate**              | **Interval**                  | **Model Handling**                | **Lag Period** |
| -------------------------- | ----------------------------- | --------------------------------- | -------------- |
| **Hemoglobin, Creatinine** | Every 30–90 days post-index   | Marginal Structural Model (MSM)   | 7–14 days      |
| **New infections, VTE**    | Continuous event flagging     | MSM + time-updated indicator      | 7 days         |
| **Treatment adherence**    | Re-evaluated monthly          | Cumulative PDC/MPR (time-updated) | 0 days         |
| **Cytogenetic markers**    | Re-tested yearly if available | Static for MSMs                   | N/A            |
> 📌 **Marginal Structural Models (MSMs)** will adjust for time-varying confounding using inverse probability weighting of treatment.
---
### 🟧 **Lagged Covariates**
Covariates influencing outcome **must be measured prior to exposure/outcome** to preserve temporal directionality.
| **Covariate**       | **Lag**    | **Purpose**                              |
| ------------------- | ---------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| **Neutropenia**     | 7-day lag  | Reflects treatment-induced effects       |
| **Hospitalization** | 14-day lag | Avoid reverse causation by outcome onset |
| **Supportive meds** | 30-day lag | Prevents post-outcome confounding        |
> ⏳ **Lag windows are applied to protect causal ordering** in exposure-outcome estimation.
---
## 🧪 **Missing Data Imputation Thresholds**
| **Covariate Type**      | **Imputation Method**            | **Missingness Tolerance** |
| ----------------------- | -------------------------------- | ------------------------- |
| **Labs (LOINC-coded)**  | MICE / mean if <20% missing      | Max 20%                   |
| **Demographics**        | No imputation; exclude           | 0% (required)             |
| **Comorbidities**       | Impute as "absent" if claim data | Max 10%                   |
| **Time-varying values** | LOCF or weighted mean            | Max 25%                   |
> 🚨 **If >25% missing in key covariates**, sensitivity analysis using **pattern mixture models** or **complete-case analysis** is required.
---
## ✅ **Conclusion**
| Strategy         | Key Design Rule                                        |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Static**       | Baseline covariates use 1-year lookback                |
| **Time-varying** | MSMs adjust dynamic traits over follow-up              |
| **Lagged**       | Ensure causal timing with 7–30 day lags                |
| **Missingness**  | Thresholds set to 10–25%, with imputation plan defined |
